programme.title: Today's Top Stories
programme.desc: All the news and info you need to know following election night.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.desc: The latest news in Canada and around the world.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: What do you need to know about the world around you? Heather Hiscox takes the helm as the anchor for this weekday-morning news program to tell you. Joining her is Colleen Jones with weather and sports info., and Danielle Bochove with all you need to know about the business world.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.desc: All the news and info you need to know following election night.

programme.title: Power & Politics
programme.desc: David Cochrane hosts special coverage of the latest federal election news.

programme.title: W5
programme.desc: W5 investigates the identity of a would-be bank robber who exploded in a dramatic heist.

programme.title: CTV National News with Omar Sachedina
programme.sub-title: CTV National News for Monday, April 28, 2025
programme.desc: CTV National News with Omar Sachedina is Canada's #1 national newscast

programme.title: CTV News: Your Canada
programme.desc: Stories that showcase what makes Canada unique.

programme.title: CTV News Special: Election 2025
programme.desc: Omar Sachedina and Vassy Kapelos bring you special coverage of the 2025 federal election.

programme.title: W5: Avery Haines Investigates
programme.desc: Avery rides atop Mexico’s “death train” through narco territory, documenting the harrowing journey migrants take in search of a better life.

programme.desc: W5 investigates the identity of a would-be bank robber who exploded in a dramatic heist.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Lynda Reeves; tech expert Nicholas Montgomery.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Chef Rocco Dispirito; hair stylist Marc Anthony.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: A family affected by the tornado in Goderich gets a complete makeover.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Natural ways to treat infertility; saving money.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Actor John Lithgow; Jonathan and Drew Scott.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Dr. Marla Shapiro's battle over breast cancer; Nadine Mercey and Brian Daly ("Paranormal Homes Investigation").

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Michelle Mawby; pumpkin treats.

programme.title: The Marilyn Denis Show
programme.desc: Parenting expert Ann Douglas discusses children's sleep issues and solutions; plus what men notice right away when they meet a single woman.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Jessica makes fair-inspired desserts, including classic funnel cake.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern creates her favourite sweet and salty treats.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern bakes delicious desserts for a galaxy-inspired party.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: It’s all about street food in this Vietnamese episode.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Joanna Chery shows us popular recipes from Athens.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Forget your passport and tear up the takeout menu – One World Kitchen will help you master the world’s hottest cuisines, Canadian style.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Mary Tang cooks up Cantonese recipes perfect for days when you need comfort food.

programme.title: Cook Like a Chef
programme.desc: Chef Derek Dammann demonstrates techniques for cooking with stonefruit.

programme.title: Cook Like a Chef
programme.desc: Chef Natalia Machado demonstrates techniques for creating delicious beef dishes.

programme.title: Cook Like a Chef
programme.desc: Chef Katie Ardington demonstrates techniques for baking and cooking with chocolate.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: A Banquet Fit for an Emperor
programme.desc: Vietnamese expert Lisa Nguyen shows us recipes that are fit for a royal dinner party.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Joanna Chery shows us intriguing recipes from the Greek island of Crete.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Nuts about Walnuts, Pine Nuts and Pistachios
programme.desc: Host Jasmin Ibrahim shares her favourite nutty recipes.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Mary Tang shows us three mouth-watering dishes using one of her favorite ingredients.

programme.title: Let's Brunch
programme.desc: Brunch is the most important meal of the weekend! Join host Vanessa Gianfrancesco as she whips up healthy and delicious recipes that’ll...

programme.title: Let's Brunch
programme.desc: Host Vanessa Gianfrancesco whips up her twist on a traditional pancake brunch with savoury, sweet and crispy recipes.

programme.title: Let's Brunch
programme.desc: Champagne, lobster, and homemade scones, oh my! Vanessa Gianfrancesco cooks up an indulgent brunch.

programme.title: One Big Recipe
programme.desc: Set in Chef Kimberly Lallouz’s test kitchen, she offers step-by-step tips to pulling off a four-course dinner for six, all in one afternoon.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Mary's leftover Easter candy brownies; colourful condo refresh; clever wardrobe hacks; purse trends; car organizing hacks for road trips.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Mary's coconut dahl; stylish sustainable fashion; must-have sustainable beauty brands; innovative new tech trends; nutrition myth busting.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Mary challenges two bakers to create stunning spring cakes; Mary's classic carrot cake; miso - the magic baking ingredient.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Long hair transformations; modern ways to decorate with vintage finds; Mary's chicken cordon bleu update; styling old clothes to feel new.

programme.title: Mary's Kitchen Crush
programme.desc: It’s a burger bonanza with Mary Berg treating her husband Aaron’s friends to fast and flavourful midweek feast.

programme.title: Mary's Kitchen Crush
programme.desc: It’s pulled pork to the rescue as Mary Berg makes magic with a versatile meal for her married friends who have very different tastes.

programme.title: Mary's Kitchen Crush
programme.desc: Mary Berg makes magic on short notice when her friend Sarah pays a surprise visit, serving up a fridge clean-out chicken pot pie.

programme.title: Mary's Kitchen Crush
programme.desc: Mary Berg is her own guest of honour so she sears up some delicious salmon for her dinner for one.

programme.title: Mary's Kitchen Crush
programme.desc: Mary is up for the challenge of impressing her MasterChef Canada former competitor, Veronica, with some next-level comfort food.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary offers four exciting ways to expand your chicken repertoire so it will never be boring or dry again!

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary settles your nerves for date night by serving up four recipes sure to impress your special guest while you maintain your chill.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary makes clean-up a breeze by whipping up four one-pot recipes that will leave you wondering, where the heck is the mess?

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary gives a quick crash course in French cuisine with recipes that sound fancy but demystify classic techniques.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shows how versatile meal planning can be with four recipes that future-you will thank you for.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Leftovers can seem uninspiring, but Mary gives them new life by showing how to use them as ingredients in brand new recipes.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Mary's leftover Easter candy brownies; colourful condo refresh; clever wardrobe hacks; purse trends; car organizing hacks for road trips.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Mary's coconut dahl; stylish sustainable fashion; must-have sustainable beauty brands; innovative new tech trends; nutrition myth busting.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Paying customers will soon be launching into space; tourist revenue is projected to be a major stepping stone.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The ESA-JAXA joint Bepi-Colombo Mission is moments away from launch. This mission will shed light on Mercury.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The return of asteroid material to Earth will aid scientists in the development in planetary defense systems.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Gaia and TESS will be joined by the James Webb Telescope. They will open up a whole new vista into space.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: This is the latest in Martian exploration, not just from orbit but also from rovers on the ground.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Tiny Satellites are growing in main stream popularity, and are being used for interplanetary missions as well.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: It appears that Einstein is once again triumphant as we discover the latest in deep space observations.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Japanese fertile lands all attest of the immeasurable wealth hidden beneath the brimstone giants.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Volcanoes have been ripping the earth apart. Are we on the verge of the next Pompeii tragedy?

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Volcanologists are ready to face any challenge to quench their thirst for knowledge

programme.title: Faster Than Light: the Dream of Interstellar Flight
programme.desc: Next-generation rocket technology is fueling the dream of traveling across the void.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The NASA Mariner 10 mission used the technique to swing by Venus to target Mercury.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: We have been observing Earth for 40 years now, in the last 20 we have focus with intensity on the planet.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Stars collide creating monsters of light and energy. Space is a rough place to be.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: In space we are merely cheating gravity. Now this fundamental universal force is slowly giving up its secrets.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The interface between Earth and space is the Ionosphere, a region of rarified gas and charged particles.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: New satellites are finding that climate change is accelerating and they have the data to prove it.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.sub-title: Set Course for the Heart of the Sun
programme.desc: The NASA's Parker Solar Probe Mission hopes to reveal the source of the solar winds that affect us on Earth.

programme.title: Battle For the Moon
programme.desc: After the Gemini program, America embarked on the Apollo program.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: All around the earth, crowds pray and worship volcanoes that are sacred to them.

programme.title: The Saturn V Story
programme.desc: This is the story of the most powerful machine ever built and a nation who watched on with hope, together.

programme.title: Hubble: Eye in the Sky
programme.desc: In this beautiful show, viewers get an inside look at the challenges of operating the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: Our World is on Fire
programme.desc: Climate change is now a crisis, and our world is crying out. Examine some possible remedies to the emergency.

programme.title: SuperTornado: Anatomy of a Megadisaster
programme.desc: A look at Joplin, Missouri five years after one of the worst natural disasters in U.S. history razed the town.

programme.title: When We Were Apollo
programme.desc: Take an intimate and personal look at Apollo through the lives and experiences of its most inspiring figures.

programme.title: Solar Superstorms: Journey to the Center of the Sun
programme.desc: As technology and electrical grids have spread, big solar storms pose a threat equal to the worst hurricanes.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Paying customers will soon be launching into space; tourist revenue is projected to be a major stepping stone.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The ESA-JAXA joint Bepi-Colombo Mission is moments away from launch. This mission will shed light on Mercury.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The return of asteroid material to Earth will aid scientists in the development in planetary defense systems.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Gaia and TESS will be joined by the James Webb Telescope. They will open up a whole new vista into space.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: This is the latest in Martian exploration, not just from orbit but also from rovers on the ground.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Tiny Satellites are growing in main stream popularity, and are being used for interplanetary missions as well.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: It appears that Einstein is once again triumphant as we discover the latest in deep space observations.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Japanese fertile lands all attest of the immeasurable wealth hidden beneath the brimstone giants.

programme.title: Living with Volcanoes
programme.desc: Volcanoes have been ripping the earth apart. Are we on the verge of the next Pompeii tragedy?

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: All over the world men and women fight for the protection of nature reserves and endangered species. This is their story, a story of bravery and stamina. We visit scientists in Central Europe who monitor the offspring of the rare lynx nearly 200 years after these elusive cats were extinct in the wild. We follow the work of the moose detective in Sweden on the island of Oland who tries to solve the riddle of increasing numbers of dead moose calves. In the heart of Africa one woman and her guards try to protect rare lowland gorillas and forest elephants but the increasing bush meat trade and the ongoing ivory smuggling makes their work extremely dangerous. Last but not least we meet US conservationists who rescue manatees in Florida - they have all dedicated their life to make a change. Without their work our world would be poorer.

programme.title: Elephants: Back to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Elephants: Back to the Wild: The Orphanage of Elephants - Nairobi Nationalpark
programme.desc: We follow the rescue of a baby elephant. Once rescued, it will be transported by plane to the breeding station and raised with love and commitment. In order to satisfy the little pachyderm’s urge to move while in the breeding station, there is sometimes an adorable football match between the animals.

programme.title: Hummingbirds: Jewelled Messengers
programme.desc: Hummingbirds live at the limits of what is possible. They have the highest metabolism with a heart rate of 1,200 beats per minute of any vertebrate. Their wings beat 50 times a second. They can hover on the spot, and even fly backwards. To our eyes, they are just a blur. But with the aid of high-speed cameras we can slow down their hyperactive lives and look into their secret world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife film-makers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. In Alaska a cunning grizzly threatens the son of Andreas Kieling, on the remote Wrangel Island in northern-east Siberia his colleague Uwe Anders is taken by surprise by a a polar bear, and off the coast of South Africa a diving-team is unexpectedly surrounded by white sharks. But the life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces, life-threatening diseases and failing technical equipment: Deep in the vast rainforests of West Africa cameraman Klaus Scheurich nearly dies of malaria, and in South India a film team narrowly escapes death in a hot-air balloon accident. But they also meet remarkable people such as a zoo keeper in Cameroon who feels at ease playing with half-grown and adult gorillas - the most powerful of all great apes. The first episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife film-makers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. A cunning grizzly grabs a salmon off Andreas Kieling's fishing rod, in the depths of the Amazon River a diving team has a spooky encounter with piranhas, in the Mediterranean Sea the currents nearly push Thomas Behrend into the mouths of angry sperm whales defending a newborn - and cameramen who want to film jaguars in the dense jungle of South America become easy prey for the well camouflaged cats. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces and failing technical equipment: In the swamps of North Australia violent storms hold up the crossing of tearing rivers, a volcano in the South Seas spits burning lumps of lava into the base camp, and in Siberia a camera team breaks through the ice and has to camp at minus 30°C in the middle of nowhere. The second episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife-filmmakers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. In the Kruger National Park Josef Jozefowicz steps into a lionesses' den to film her cubs, in Alaska Andreas Kieling mingles with giant elk bulls in order to film them in action, and in Florida his colleague Zoltan Török gets very close to an alligator mother guarding its nest. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces, and failing technical equipment: the strong and tearing current of the Yukon River throws Andreas Kieling's boat onto a gravel bank, in the Democratic Republik of Congo camerawoman Heike Grebe is separated from her colleagues in the dense rainforest, in Kamchatka a film team only just escapes a gigantic landslide, and in Finland a hot-air balloon catches fire and becomes the nightmare of Oliver Goetzl and Ivo Nörenberg. The last episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife film-makers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Jacques Cousteau's Legacy - Return To The Undersea World
programme.desc: A composition of wildlife footage, archive material and elaborate re-enactments results in a brilliant reconstruction of the life of famed marine biologist and environmental pioneer Jacques Cousteau. On the trail of the most famous oceanographer of the 20th Century, the team spent two years filming original locations on three different continents, in four different oceans. The film charts how Cousteau’s outlook on the environment changed throughout his many decades at sea around the globe. On his personal journey to enlightenment, he changed from a man who killed sharks in hate and thoughtlessly blew up coral reefs, to a radical environmentalist.

programme.title: The Secret of Migrating Birds - Tryst at the Red Sea
programme.desc: Each year in spring, Israel witnesses a unique spectacle. Many millions of migrating birds cross the land bridge between Africa, Asia and Europe.Their first major roosting place after an exhausting nonstop flight over the African deserts is in southern Israel – a vital stopover. Here they replenish their energy reserves. The flocks of migrating birds are pursued by flocks of ornithologists. Hundreds of birders flood into the port city of Eilat on the Red Sea to take part in the Migration Festival. For a week, Israel's southernmost coastal resort is home to international scientists and amateur birders.

programme.title: Unnatural Selection
programme.desc: The wings of cliff swallows in Nebraska are getting shorter – while female turtles in Chesapeake Bay are getting larger. On the Galapagos Islands, two species of finches are developing back into one. Increasing numbers of peregrine falcons are living in urban areas – and have adapted their behaviour: thanks to the city lights, they have learned to hunt at night.

programme.title: Wild Prairie Man
programme.desc: Located in the Northern Great Plains of Canada, Grasslands National Park has been a 20 year odyssey for wildlife photographer James R. Page. For James, critters are more interesting than people and he shares some of his iconic images and experiences from this endangered ecosystem. We showcase the parks wilderness that is dominated by prairie light and dark sky, the wildlife, prairie dogs, mesmerizing landscapes, and bisons. We immerse ourselves in the wild habitat of Grasslands National Park for over a year capturing some never before scenes in the Wild Prairie. James R. Page's connection to the land and the wildlife provides an unforgettable journey into the heart of this endangered ecosystem, while Park experts help us understand its complexity.

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: In a land of dangers, plant-eating animals are always at risk, and never more so than at the time of their birth; out on the open they are vulnerable and there are few places to hide. But luckily some are born to run …

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: It's a water world and life couldn't survive without it. And thanks to it, a mind blowing diversity of creatures thrive beneath the surface. Born to enjoy the riches underwater they must master their body's adaptations, learn to find food, to escape danger and above all become expert swimmers! Whether it's in rivers, on beaches or in the deep ocean, all must overcome the obstacles and fulfil their destiny, all are born to swim! Of all the habitats on earth, the ocean is perhaps the most challenging. Here salt, temperatures, currents and predators can make life difficult; it's not an obvious place to want to bring up your babies… But even in these waters generation after generation beats the odds. They have overcome the challenges and become some of the most beautiful and graceful of creatures. But any baby born to swim has a lot to learn… All over the world there are creatures born to swim, and though humans are not, we seem determined to join them. Perhaps we are envious of their grace. Maybe we are all water babies at heart

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: Baby animals beguile with their innocent charm. But while some remain harmless, others have a deep-rooted instinct. It may take weeks or months of training to hone their skills. Their landscapes and lifestyles maybe very different, but these babies are born to hunt.

programme.title: The Cape of Wild Animals, Episode 5
programme.desc: The movie focuses on committed animal rights activists, who fight for the lives of their charges every day, in diverse protection projects and rescue centres along the South African Garden Route.

programme.title: White Lions: Born Wild
programme.sub-title: White Lions: Born Wild - Episode 1: Against all Odds
programme.desc: The two sisters are born as white as snow in May 2009 in South Africa’s Kruger Park. Growing up in this vast, wild wilderness they must not only overcome the same survival challenges that all young lion cubs face, they must also overcome the threats their high visibility brings. In the African bush, anything unusual stands out and is often eliminated by eagle-eyed predators. Will our cubs survive? The odds are stacked against them – 80 percent of all newborn lion cubs don’t make it to adulthood.

programme.title: White Lions: Born Wild
programme.sub-title: White Lions: Born Wild - Episode 2: Fight for Survival
programme.desc: ‘White Lions – Born Wild’ is a true wildlife story, with strong characters and closely studied behaviour. We follow the cubs’ progress as they embark on the pathway to adulthood, learning to hunt, learning to avoid danger, learning to live in their white coats. This is a strong behaviour-led film, a coming-of-age story of a very rare and unusual pair of lions. Through their eyes, we learn what it is really like to be a lion in Africa.

programme.title: Snowy Owl: Queen of the North
programme.desc: No other owls are capable of surviving the extreme conditions, whereas the snowy owl is able to hunt through six months of bright sunshine and six months of twilight. This film follows a pair of snowy owls as they breed on the tundra of the High Arctic, 300 miles inside the Arctic Circle. Lemming numbers are low this year. Five owlets hatch and they face a struggle if any are going to survive.

programme.title: King of the Seas - Sea Eagle
programme.desc: The times where eagles have been seen as varmints are over. They are now perceived as an integral part of the food cycle. Due to the fact that the habitats of the sea eagles in Germany are largely found in the north-eastern regions that are rich in water, the movie accompanies the eagles to an island, home to hundreds of sea ravens, where they rob the baby sea ravens of their food. They seem to always profit from the successes of the fellow creatures in their habitats. This film introduces you to the expert poachers of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: King of the Mountains - Golden Eagle
programme.desc: Aerial shots of mating Golden eagles, a clash between a Golden and a White-tailed eagle, and an intimate glimpse into their nesting grounds combine to create a spectacular film. Tracking these majestic birds reveals the wild nature of their hunting grounds, showcasing the diverse animal and plant life that share their habitat with the 'king of the skies.' Viewers are brought up close as the eagle effortlessly glides over forests and canyons, abruptly diving in a breathtaking pursuit of prey. Witnessing these birds in their natural habitats is witnessing them at their finest. Embark on a magnificent journey through the skies and landscapes with us.

programme.title: Hummingbirds: Jewelled Messengers
programme.desc: Hummingbirds live at the limits of what is possible. They have the highest metabolism with a heart rate of 1,200 beats per minute of any vertebrate. Their wings beat 50 times a second. They can hover on the spot, and even fly backwards. To our eyes, they are just a blur. But with the aid of high-speed cameras we can slow down their hyperactive lives and look into their secret world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife film-makers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. In Alaska a cunning grizzly threatens the son of Andreas Kieling, on the remote Wrangel Island in northern-east Siberia his colleague Uwe Anders is taken by surprise by a a polar bear, and off the coast of South Africa a diving-team is unexpectedly surrounded by white sharks. But the life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces, life-threatening diseases and failing technical equipment: Deep in the vast rainforests of West Africa cameraman Klaus Scheurich nearly dies of malaria, and in South India a film team narrowly escapes death in a hot-air balloon accident. But they also meet remarkable people such as a zoo keeper in Cameroon who feels at ease playing with half-grown and adult gorillas - the most powerful of all great apes. The first episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife film-makers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. A cunning grizzly grabs a salmon off Andreas Kieling's fishing rod, in the depths of the Amazon River a diving team has a spooky encounter with piranhas, in the Mediterranean Sea the currents nearly push Thomas Behrend into the mouths of angry sperm whales defending a newborn - and cameramen who want to film jaguars in the dense jungle of South America become easy prey for the well camouflaged cats. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces and failing technical equipment: In the swamps of North Australia violent storms hold up the crossing of tearing rivers, a volcano in the South Seas spits burning lumps of lava into the base camp, and in Siberia a camera team breaks through the ice and has to camp at minus 30°C in the middle of nowhere. The second episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife-filmmakers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. In the Kruger National Park Josef Jozefowicz steps into a lionesses' den to film her cubs, in Alaska Andreas Kieling mingles with giant elk bulls in order to film them in action, and in Florida his colleague Zoltan Török gets very close to an alligator mother guarding its nest. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces, and failing technical equipment: the strong and tearing current of the Yukon River throws Andreas Kieling's boat onto a gravel bank, in the Democratic Republik of Congo camerawoman Heike Grebe is separated from her colleagues in the dense rainforest, in Kamchatka a film team only just escapes a gigantic landslide, and in Finland a hot-air balloon catches fire and becomes the nightmare of Oliver Goetzl and Ivo Nörenberg. The last episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife film-makers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Jacques Cousteau's Legacy - Return To The Undersea World
programme.desc: A composition of wildlife footage, archive material and elaborate re-enactments results in a brilliant reconstruction of the life of famed marine biologist and environmental pioneer Jacques Cousteau. On the trail of the most famous oceanographer of the 20th Century, the team spent two years filming original locations on three different continents, in four different oceans. The film charts how Cousteau’s outlook on the environment changed throughout his many decades at sea around the globe. On his personal journey to enlightenment, he changed from a man who killed sharks in hate and thoughtlessly blew up coral reefs, to a radical environmentalist.

programme.title: The Secret of Migrating Birds - Tryst at the Red Sea
programme.desc: Each year in spring, Israel witnesses a unique spectacle. Many millions of migrating birds cross the land bridge between Africa, Asia and Europe.Their first major roosting place after an exhausting nonstop flight over the African deserts is in southern Israel – a vital stopover. Here they replenish their energy reserves. The flocks of migrating birds are pursued by flocks of ornithologists. Hundreds of birders flood into the port city of Eilat on the Red Sea to take part in the Migration Festival. For a week, Israel's southernmost coastal resort is home to international scientists and amateur birders.

programme.title: Rewild
programme.desc: As biodiversity collapses, rewilding offers hope: from reintroducing jaguars to planting forests, global efforts restore ecosystems and fight climate change.

programme.title: Masters Of The Wild
programme.desc: Animals adapt to hostile landscapes using specialized bodies, diets, and behaviors, such as scorpions

programme.title: Lionfish - New Pirates Of The Caribbean
programme.desc: The documentary explores the lion fish, introduced in the 1990s, posing a threat to native species

programme.title: Wild Faces Of Switzerland
programme.desc: Graubünden in winter means there is a struggle for survival because of the cold.

programme.title: Animal Special Forces
programme.desc: Certain animals possess advanced senses beyond basic ones

programme.title: Trees Of Life
programme.desc: See how humble plants triggered Earth’s greatest transformation

programme.title: Hamilton’s Journey - Manatees in a New Light
programme.desc: A manatee has to be extremely unlucky to be given a name. Hamilton is a living example of this.

programme.title: Adopted By Dolphins - Flipper's Family Secrets
programme.desc: Researchers are accepted as companions by a population of bottlenose dolphins in the Red Sea

programme.title: Beak & Brain - Genius Birds From Down Under
programme.desc: These child prodigies of the animal kingdom have feathers and beaks - and very special skills!

programme.title: Germany's Mystic Forest
programme.desc: Explore impenetrable forests, caves, rivers, and wild landscapes. Discover diverse wildlife like wolves, deer, and rare species in Germany's Wild Amazon.

programme.title: Rewild
programme.desc: As biodiversity collapses, rewilding offers hope: from reintroducing jaguars to planting forests, global efforts restore ecosystems and fight climate change.

programme.title: Masters Of The Wild
programme.desc: Animals adapt to hostile landscapes using specialized bodies, diets, and behaviors, such as scorpions

programme.title: Lionfish - New Pirates Of The Caribbean
programme.desc: The documentary explores the lion fish, introduced in the 1990s, posing a threat to native species

programme.title: Wild Faces Of Switzerland
programme.desc: Learn about four different regions of Switzerland.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Animals like toads, moths, snakes, and spiders have poison glands

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: The archer fish uses a blowgun to capture insects, while the jawfish defends itself by throwing boulders.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Cuttlefish and squid use black fluid to hide from predators, while skunks also use scent glands for defense.

programme.title: To the Ends of the Earth Oceans
programme.desc: Todd Gustafson, a wildlife photographer, shares insights on sea life, showcasing stunning underwater creatures

programme.title: Hunters Of The Skies
programme.desc: Hawks, buzzards, and eagles are powerful birds with diverse hunting strategies, conquering various habitats

programme.title: Wild Slovakia
programme.desc: Wild Slovakia is a documentary film showcasing Slovakia's diverse wildlife, habitats, and ecosystems

programme.title: Chandani - The Daughter Of The Elephant Whisperer
programme.desc: Chandani, a girl in a male profession, takes on the role of a Mahout, supported by ranger Mohammed

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef 4K
programme.desc: Dive into the Great Barrier Reef in stunning 4K, exploring vibrant coral, turtles, and fish in one of Earth's most diverse natural wonders.

programme.title: Wild Morocco Monkeys And Snakes
programme.desc: Documentary highlights Morocco's unique wildlife, featuring Barbary apes & rare reptiles in their habitats.

programme.title: Megeti
programme.desc: A lone Ethiopian Wolf battles the elements and other predators as she tries to unite with a pack.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Les learns the art of poison arrow hunting, tracks porcupine, and hikes the great sand dunes of the Kalahari

programme.title: What If
programme.desc: Take an epic journey through hypothetical worlds and possibilities.

programme.title: Crazy Creatures
programme.desc: Evolution has crafted some of the strangest beings you can imagine, and it's all been done for a reason.

programme.title: Survivorman: Bigfoot
programme.desc: Les Stroud seeks to uncover the truth about this elusive and quite possibly fictitious creature.

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Kalahari Meerkats
programme.desc: Recently Priscilla bought an adopted boy pup from a rival gang to the Gosa Burrow. Unfortunately his acceptance into the Gosa group was far from ideal. His life did not get any easier after this

programme.title: Hidden Spain
programme.desc: Spain is found at the crossroads between the Mediterranean and the Atlantic, resulting in diverse landscapes, ancient cultures and magnificent wildlife.

programme.title: Fighting for a Mate
programme.desc: Animals face daily battles to protect themselves and secure the resources they need to survive. Every battle is a trade-off between the risk to health and the prizes awarded to the victors. Each animal fights with a unique set of tools, from display structures and elaborate body language to claws, jaws and antlers. And few fights are as fuelled as those during breeding season the ultimate trophy for the brave fighter is, of course, a chance to pass on the fighting genes to the next generation.

programme.title: Europe's Wilderness
programme.desc: With antlers that can grow up to 2 meters in size and can consume 10kg of grass, young trees and bark a day the Elk is the largest deep spieces in the world. With their habitat the dense, poorly accessible forests and moorlands, we see the coumminities of Elk driven up by climate change.

programme.title: Kalahari Meerkats
programme.desc: The Gosa Gang have recently moved to a new home known as Fortress burrow. It is close to the Gosa Dam where a rival family of meerkats live.In the quest for territorial dominance there has been a major meerkat standoff. This has resulted in both gangs losing a member of their tribe.

programme.title: Kalahari Meerkats
programme.desc: Summer has arrived in the kalahari. It has been three weeks since the last rains and the desert is flourishing. A large group of giraffe have moved into the area which has been unsettling for the gang

programme.title: Kalahari Meerkats
programme.desc: Finally the two rival gangs meet and a big battle ensues. The Gosa Gang are on their way home from the Whispering Forest and bump into their bitter enemies head on. In an attempt to get past them and move over the mountain a full on war breaks out

programme.title: Nuts about Squirrels
programme.desc: Squirrels have conquered tundra, deserts, forests and more, developing ingenious life skills along the way, from stashing nuts to fighting snakes.

programme.title: History of Life on Earth Journey To The Land
programme.desc: As the first landmasses emerged from prehistoric seas, animals took their first steps on dry land. The explosion of life that followed changed the world forever.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: We can't compare anything on earth to the biggest things known in space.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: A constellation is a group of stars that are connected together to form a figure.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Dark Matter and Dark Energy make up 96% of the Universe.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: New discoveries are creating a fundamental rethinking of our solar system.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: In this episode, two kinds of star clusters in the galaxy are explored.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Thousands of objects both natural and manmade plummet through our atmosphere.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Celestial forces prowl the Universe and threaten man's very existence.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: We can't compare anything on earth to the biggest things known in space.
